text: A test exercising the new or changed behavior was written before the production code for that behavior existed (for the bugfix: the reproducing test was written before the fix was applied).

The test was actually executed and shown failing before the implementation or fix was written — the failing run's output appears, and the failure is the expected missing-behavior failure, not a syntax, import, or collection error.
The implementation written after the red run is minimal for the tests at hand — no speculative options, parameters, or features beyond what the tests demand.
The full test command was re-run after implementing and shown passing, with no other tests broken and no errors or warnings riding along in the output.
Tests assert on real code behavior (no mocking of the unit under test, no asserting on mock wiring), and edge cases are covered by tests rather than by claims.
